name: App Shell
summary: Chrome layout primitive that frames a page: a (sticky) header with a brand lockup and a header-controls region, an optional nav landmark, the main content region (preceded by a skip link), and an optional footer. Built for the #870 must-build set so the #865 WE-docs chrome dogfood can mount it in-document (mode C). Packaged as the persistent light-DOM `<we-app-shell>` custom element (#1617, B-mechanism per block-standard §7) whose reactive `config` property re-renders the shell; with `slotMain` it projects the element's authored light-DOM children into `<main>` (a real `<slot>` only projects in shadow DOM).

landmarkRoles.pattern: header / nav / main / footer landmark elements structure the page for assistive technology
landmarkRoles.adoption: App Shell emits native <header>, <nav aria-label>, <main>, <footer> landmark regions

skipLink.pattern: A skip link as the first focusable element lets keyboard users bypass repeated chrome
skipLink.adoption: App Shell renders a skip link targeting the focusable <main> region

positionSticky.pattern: position: sticky pins the header to the top on scroll without JavaScript
positionSticky.adoption: Sticky header is the default; opt out via config
